MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF VWR INTERNATIONAL, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. VWR International, LLC ("VWR") moves for summary judgment, and not summary adjudication. VWR has not its initial burden of proof on summary judgment. In its motion, VWR addresses only the contractual indemnity and declaratory relief causes of action, and not the partial and total equitable indemnity causes of action. "[A] plaintiff bears the burden of persuasion that 'each element of' the 'cause of action' in question has been 'proved,' and hence that 'there is no defense' thereto. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c, subd. (o)(1).)" (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Thus, VWR has not its initial burden of persuasion. Further, VWR admits that a triable issue of fact exists as to whether it is entitled to partial or total equitable indemnity. (Motion, P&A, p. 1:16-18, 15:15-16; Reply, p. 1:26 - 2:2.) Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael P. Pulliam, Drinker Biddle & Reath, 415.591.7634, appearing for the Plaintiff;
David M. Glaspy, Law Offices of McGivney Kluger & Glaspy, 925.947.1300, appearing for the Defendant.

Ruling: A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CSC SCIENTIFIC COMPANY, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. The request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Stipulated Protective Order signed. Mr. Glaspy is to prepare the order. Ernst Halperin, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
